findings.finding: is_limit_line false-positive on the substring 'limit reached'. A claude pane that legitimately prints text containing 'limit reached' (agent output discussing rate limits, docs, editing/reviewing code that mentions it, or echoing user text) flips the badge to `limited` spuriously. The unit test only guards 'rate limited by the API' (no 'reached'), but 'rate limit reached' WOULD match.
findings.evidence: l.contains("limit reached") || l.contains("out of extra usage")
findings.fix: Self-heals within one poll cycle since an active pane floods >LIMIT_RESUME_BYTES (4096) quickly, and it only sets an informational badge on claude panes — no data/security impact. If tightened, anchor on the fuller CC banner ('Claude usage limit reached') or require a line-start match. Acceptable as-is per the documented endpoint backstop.

findings.finding: The `limited` resume-clear needs >LIMIT_RESUME_BYTES (4096) of post-limit output. A genuine reset whose resumed turn emits a very small total (agent prints a couple bytes and stops) would keep the pane pinned to `limited`. Exited panes are safe (compute checks `exited` before `limited`), so it cannot get stuck across a close — only across an unusually tiny resume.
findings.evidence: if t.limited.load(Ordering::Relaxed) && t.bytes_since_limit.fetch_add(bytes as u64, Ordering::Relaxed) + bytes as u64 > LIMIT_RESUME_BYTES
findings.fix: Acceptable: a real post-reset agent turn floods far more than 4096 bytes; the exited-outranks-limited ordering prevents a permanent stick. No change required.

findings.finding: Antispam re-arm keys off `resets_at`; when the API omits it, `win` collapses to the constant '-' so a level fired once never re-arms even across a genuinely new window. Only reachable if the usage endpoint stops sending resets_at, which is not observed in practice.
findings.fix: Acceptable degradation and explicitly documented ('a changed reset ... re-arms'). No change required. (Note: prefix collision between profiles e.g. '.claude-cc1' vs '.claude-cc10' is NOT possible — the \x1f separator after the profile segment prevents starts_with/ends_with overlap; verified.)

summary: TOKEN TRACE (highest priority) — CLEAN. Full flow of the OAuth access token: read_access_token(cred_path) parses claudeAiOauth.accessToken from disk and returns Option<String> (limits.rs:84-92, no log). poll_profile binds it to local `token` and passes &token to fetch_usage (l.189-192) — it is not emitted, stored, or put in any struct. fetch_usage(token:&str) uses it ONLY as format!("Bearer {token}") on the Authorization header to USAGE_URL=https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage over TLS (l.104); every error path maps to Err(u16 status) or Err(0) with NO token in the value, and the ureq::Error is never formatted/logged. Neither payload struct carries it: LimitsStatus = {profile,h5,d7,resets,expired}, LimitsAlert = {profile,window,level,utilization,resets_at}. grep of the whole file confirms zero println!/eprintln!/log::/dbg!/tracing! — the only matches are doc comments and legit .to_string() on profile/window/numbers. The token is sent to exactly one host (api.anthropic.com) and nowhere else. Verdict: no leak. (Minor defense-in-depth: the String isn't zeroized after use, but the same secret already sits in plaintext .credentials.json and is used by the user's statusline, so this is not a regression.) ANTISPAM — correct: take_alert fires each of 85/99 at most once per (profile,window,resets_at); the retain(prefix && !ends_with(suffix)) drops only the prior window's levels on a resets_at change, re-arming both thresholds; the \x1f separators make cc1/cc10-style prefix collisions impossible; unit test covers fire-once + re-arm + independent (profile,window). FETCH_USAGE — non-200/timeout/transport are clean skips (Err(code)/Err(0)); 401 emits expired=true with no refresh; 8s timeout_global bounds the whole call on its own poll thread, so a hang delays only subsequent profile polls, never the UI or PTY. STATE MACHINE — compute orders exited > limited > hook/activity, so limited cannot stick past a pane close; on_limit no-ops for non-claude/untracked panes (tool=="claude" + get() guard); the fetch_add+bytes>threshold clear is written and read by the single per-session PTY reader thread (poll thread only reads the atomic), so no atomicity/off-by-one bug. POLL THREAD — lock poisoning handled via unwrap_or_else(into_inner); config toggle checked each iteration; first poll after one interval (no startup block). Three low/informational notes recorded, none blocking. Claim of correct-and-safe holds.
